summary.quantification_method: Relative quantification of positional isomers via fragment ion intensities

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison by phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitination, or activation markers.
evidence: determine the quantities of the same protein modified differentially by PTMs, creating so-called positional isomers
The method is tested by investigating positional isomers of Ubiquitin mixed in known concentrations
Application to multiphosphorylated Bora provides an estimation of the so far unknown stoichiometry of the known set of phosphosites
The catalytic subunit of cAMP-dependent Protein Kinase (PKA) used to in vitro phosphorylate proteins
Ataxin562–815 was phosphorylated through incubation with PKAc in 50 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM ATP pH7 for 1 h at 30 °C.

replicate_hints: Of each sample and each setting change, a set of triplicate measurements was recorded
multiplex_channel_hints: Heavy labeled ubiquitin proteoforms were synthesized by solid-phase peptide synthesis
Fmoc-labeled heavy valine (13C5, 15N) (Cortecnet) was introduced at the appropriate positions

evidence: The 37+ charge state of intact, nonphosphorylated Ataxin562–815 at 748Th and the 38+ charge state of doubly phosphorylated Ataxin562–815 at 733 Th
Mixing positional isomer A and C in a 1:1 ratio in our Ubiquitin model system

quantification_strategy: Relative quantification of positional isomers via fragment ion intensities

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment uses direct infusion and ETD fragmentation of intact proteins (top-down) to quantify relative abundances of positional isomers, which aligns most closely with a label-free quantification approach, although the data is top-down rather than bottom-up.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly focuses on top-down proteomics to characterize and quantify proteoforms and positional isomers using ETD fragmentation and a specialized 'QPI' software.
